description: Pings from the standby switch to VRRP IPs are not successful, resulting in traffic loss from the access layer to the edge.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what caveat is documented for L3 Addressing?
Feature Caveat: L3 Addressing - Pings from the standby switch to VRRP IPs are not successful, resulting in traffic loss from the access layer to the edge.

description: When no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and the client MAC is not learned then the first L2VPN DHCP client packet is dropped.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what caveat is documented for DHCP Relay?
Feature Caveat: DHCP Relay - When no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and the client MAC is not learned then the first L2VPN DHCP client packet is dropped.

description: When configuring the same BGP neighbor under both IPv4 and IPv6 address families on a router, if a maximum prefix limit is set on one address family (like IPv4), exceeding that limit can potentially impact the BGP session for the other address family (IPv6) as well.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - When configuring the same BGP neighbor under both IPv4 and IPv6 address families on a router, if a maximum prefix limit is set on one address family (like IPv4), exceeding that limit can potentially impact the BGP session for the other address family (IPv6) as well.

description: If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what caveat is documented for Activate?

Feature Caveat: Activate - If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

description: When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Classifiers - Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: For secure classifier policy modifications, HPE Aruba Networking strongly encourages a three-step process: bring down the port, make the modifications, and then bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers?

Feature Caveat: Classifiers - For secure classifier policy modifications, HPE Aruba Networking strongly encourages a three-step process: bring down the port, make the modifications, and then bring the port back up.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what caveat is documented for CMF?
Feature Caveat: CMF - No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

description: When downloading a hot-patch file using the switch WebUI, log messages may incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When downloading a hot-patch file using the switch WebUI, log messages may incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: IGMP cannot be enabled on either Loopback or GRE interfaces. IGMP and PIM is not supported on a 6-in-6 Tunnel.

description: Do not use reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services for different services. When two services use the same port, unexpected behaviors may occur. Best practice is to assign a unique port for each service across the system.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Do not use reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services for different services. When two services use the same port, unexpected behaviors may occur. Best practice is to assign a unique port for each service across the system.

description: In an environment with devices running AOS-Switch, do not enable UDLD on the same link. The UDLD session can toggle between up and down continuously when both MACsec and UDLD is enabled on the same link.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what caveat is documented for MACsec and UDLD?

Feature Caveat: MACsec and UDLD - In an environment with devices running AOS-Switch, do not enable UDLD on the same link. The UDLD session can toggle between up and down continuously when both MACsec and UDLD is enabled on the same link.

description: (for 9300S Switch Series only) WAN MACsec with a custom ether type will not be established between CX devices when there are PVOS switches in the bypass.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what caveat is documented for MACsec?
Feature Caveat: MACsec - (for 9300S Switch Series only) WAN MACsec with a custom ether type will not be established between CX devices when there are PVOS switches in the bypass.

description: In an environment with Cisco devices, when the GCM-AES-XPN-128 or GCM-AES-XPN-256 cipher suite is used for establishing the MACsec channel, the MKA policy on the Cisco device must be configured with ssci-based-on-sci.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what limitation is mentioned for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - In an environment with Cisco devices, when the GCM-AES-XPN-128 or GCM-AES-XPN-256 cipher suite is used for establishing the MACsec channel, the MKA policy on the Cisco device must be configured with ssci-based-on-sci.

description: (for 9300S Switch Series only) In an environment with a Cisco device, the Cisco device must be designated as the key server. Designating the AOS-CX as the key server results in complete traffic loss.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - (for 9300S Switch Series only) In an environment with a Cisco device, the Cisco device must be designated as the key server. Designating the AOS-CX as the key server results in complete traffic loss.

description: MACsec uses a software-based implementation to track start and stop times for secure channels and secure associations. As the implementation is software-based, the stop times for MACsec secure channel and secure associations are only updated when they are deleted and therefore never updated in the output of the show macsec status detailed command.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

description: (for 9300S Switch Series only) In an environment with Cisco and FlexFabric or H3C devices, do not update confidentiality-offset on the live channel. There can be complete traffic loss for an extended period on the MACsec channel when confidentiality-offset is updated on both ends.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

description: (for 9300S Switch Series only) In an environment with Juniper, configure no include-sci-tag under MACsec policy for data traffic forwarding.

Feature Caveat: MACsec - (for 9300S Switch Series only) In an environment with Juniper, configure no include-sci-tag under MACsec policy for data traffic forwarding.

description: Symptom: Ethernet-tag mappings are permitted only when the operational state of the EVPN VLAN-aware bundle is disabled. Scenario: In previous versions of AOS-CX, modifications to VLAN: Ethernet-tag mappings were permitted only when the operational state of the EVPN VLAN-aware bundle was disabled. In AOS-CX 10.17, this restriction has been relaxed-modifications are now allowed regardless of the operational state, provided that the resulting effective Ethernet tag does not conflict with any existing VLAN tag assignment. This ensures configuration flexibility while maintaining tag uniqueness and preventing forwarding conflicts.

description: Symptom: A Cfg_version being set to 0 results in a policy always having the in-progress message attached to it, without any changes being made to hardware resources. This will not result in a crash. The system has a low chance of getting corrupted internal data for classifier lists (ACLs, policies, etc) over time. Once this data gets corrupted, any changes to a classifier list will cause the system to crash. Scenario: Setting the cfg_version on any classifier list to 0 results in the list being perpetually stuck "in-progress". There is no specific configuration or deployment model that can cause corrupted internal data for classifier lists; this can happen randomly. Workaround: Setting cfg_version to any valid schema value other than 0 and not the current in_progress_version value will allow configurations to be processed.

description: Symptom: IP packets containing options headers bypass routed-in security policies configured with drop actions, resulting in policy enforcement failure. Scenario: When security policies are applied in the routed-in direction with drop actions configured, IP packets containing options headers are being processed by the policy engine but are circumventing the intended policy enforcement, causing these packets to be incorrectly forwarded through the system instead of being dropped as intended. Resolution: This issue has been resolved in the current implementation. Note that alternative TCAM configurations were not affected by this behavioral anomaly.

description: Symptom: The VSX daemon may become unresponsive or hang due to a deadlock condition. Scenario: This issue can occur under rare circumstances when a device with an active peer connection loses that connection and another thread simultaneously attempts to access the peer connection. Workaround: Restarting the VSX daemon resolves the issue.

description: Symptom: Interface Vxlan configuration is disabled on 9300 platform, for spine & DC-Optimized-Spine profiles from 10.15 onwards. Scenario: If Spine profile with VxLAN configs is being used before 10.15 release, then they must change the profile to Leaf before upgrading to 10.15 or higher CX versions to avoid config loss / traffic outage. Workaround: Change to Leaf profile and perform switch reboot - before upgrading to 10.15 or higher CX versions.
